_source: Build guidance (level-up priority, perks, weapons, playstyle) is derived from the user's guide: docs/battle_brothers_guide_2026.md (its 'Build Templates' quick reference and the frontline/backline/late-game perk sections). The numeric requirement thresholds are UNCHANGED and still come from WANG Chenhua's community guide (https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=1790093811) — the user's guide is prose and gives no level-11 numbers, and the calculator needs concrete targets to rate recruits.
_guidance_note: The user's guide has 5 build templates. Shield/2H/Reach/Archer/Sergeant map directly; the 1H Battle-Forged and 1H Nimble-Dodge frontlines are the guide's one-hand/Duelist and Nimble+Dodge variants. Handgonne Man and Thrower are NOT dedicated builds in the guide and are ADAPTED from its ranged and throwing-weapon notes.

guidance.playstyle: The backbone of the line and early survival. Shields grant a big defense bonus plus Shieldwall; spearwall stops wolves and other chargers. Use Rotation to pull a hurt brother back and push a fresh one forward, and Footwork to disengage cleanly. Steel Brow and Nine Lives protect the investment.

Heavy plate (250+ body) so armor soaks HP damage; Brawny keeps fatigue workable. Swings behind or beside the shield line. Berserk chains action points on kills and Fearsome saps enemy morale. Needs good Fatigue to keep swinging.

weapons: 1H cleaver / axe / mace (Duelist boosts one-hander damage)
Off-hand free for a fire/flash pot, or a shield when pressed
guidance.playstyle: The one-hand + Duelist variant of the heavy frontline: fewer sweeping hits than a two-hander but strong armor-ignoring damage on a single target, with the free off-hand for a pot or a shield. Wants higher Melee Defense than the two-hander since it has no reach.

weapons: 1H sword / cleaver + shield or free hand
guidance.playstyle: The Nimble defensive identity: light armor means incoming HP damage is reduced, and high Initiative feeds Dodge for extra Melee/Ranged Defense. A mobile frontliner that takes fewer, smaller hits — Footwork to escape when it turns.

Stands IN the line, not behind it, carrying the Banner whose resolve aura steadies nearby brothers. Rally the Troops reverses a panic spiral. Worth a full slot in mid/late game — keep him alive and your line holds when it should break.

The classic backbone: attacks from the second rank over your shields, dealing damage while safe. Reach Advantage stacks Melee Defense the more it attacks, turning it into a second tank. Arguably the best value in the game.

Same second-rank reach damage but on the Nimble identity: light armor plus Dodge, with high Initiative keeping Dodge strong all fight. Reach Advantage still makes it tanky, and it shrugs off armor-ignoring hits better than the Battle Forged version.

Deletes enemy shamans, marksmen, and low-armor priority targets before they matter. Bullseye ignores cover and obstruction penalties (mandatory); Quick Hands swaps to a melee weapon for free when lines close. Overwhelm lowers the accuracy of whatever it shoots.

weapons: Crossbow (armor penetration, no fatigue to aim)
guidance.playstyle: Crossbows punch through armor and cost no fatigue to aim, making them strong against armored priority targets. Same supporting perks as the archer; Quick Hands to swap to melee when the enemy closes.

weapons: Handgonne (area damage, hits several targets in a line)
guidance.playstyle: Adapted (the source guide doesn't cover handgonnes as a dedicated build). An area-damage firearm for line-to-line battles that can spread Overwhelm across a packed enemy front. Stands in the second row, so it wants better Ranged Defense than a bowman.

Adapted (the source guide treats throwing weapons as burst utility rather than a dedicated build). Short-range burst damage; Quick Hands lets a frontliner or backliner throw a volley then swap back to a melee weapon when the enemy arrives.
